name: Ophiotaurus

visual_attributes.morphology: creature with the front half of a bull and the back half of a serpent
visual_attributes.texture: scaly on the serpent half, smooth on the bull half
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: large, comparable to a bull in size
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: bull's head and forelimbs transitioning into a serpent's body and tail

domain_specific_visual.mythological_context: depicted in a dark, wooded setting surrounded by triple walls

functional_attributes.primary_function: subject of a prophecy with the power to defeat the gods if its innards are burned
secondary_functions: representation of mythological challenges to divine power

domain_specific_functional.role_in_titanomachy: played a part in the conflict between gods and Titans

relationships.description: Briareus attempted to burn the Ophiotaurus' innards to fulfill the prophecy
Zeus sent birds to prevent the burning of the Ophiotaurus' innards

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the vulnerability of gods and the power of prophecy

contrastive_constraints: NOT a creature with separate bull and snake forms
